prompt.description: The worker's first user-turn (string). Follow the worker-prompt template in your system prompt (§Worker prompts: directive · Context · Acceptance · Out of scope · Report).

name.description: Friendly slug for the worker (kebab-case). Should describe the outcome, not the action. Good: 'refactor-auth-tokens', 'add-billing-tests'. Bad: 'worker-1', 'fix-stuff', 'task'.

model.description: Claude model enum: 'opus' (default) | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'. Which tier fits which work → §Model. When in doubt, omit (defaults to opus).

effort.description: Reasoning effort enum. Pass ONLY for models that support it — opus and sonnet do, haiku does NOT (omit there). Which level fits which work → §Model. When in doubt, omit (defaults to xhigh).

workspaceOf.description: Spawn this worker INSIDE an existing worker's isolated worktree instead of a fresh one, for direct file access (to review, continue, or fix that worker's work). Pass the id of a worker you spawned (attaching to another orchestrator's worker is rejected). Only allowed while that worker is idle (fails with a conflict while it is busy).

collaborate.description: Give this worker the peer tools (list_peers / ask_peer / respond_to_peer) so it can consult — and be consulted by — the other collaborate workers you spawn under you (its siblings). Omit ⇒ off.

from.description: Available worker (definition) to spawn from. It pre-fills this worker's defaults (model, effort, permission mode, persistence) and frames its instructions — fields you pass explicitly still win. Omit ⇒ defaults to the general-purpose worker.

toolsAllow.description: Fence THIS worker's tools for a one-off (globs, e.g. 'Read', 'mcp__*'). Allowlist is exhaustive: anything not listed is denied. Empty/omit ⇒ inherit all tools. Wins over a `from` definition's allowlist.

toolsDeny.description: Subtract tools from THIS one-off worker (globs). Always subtracts, even from an allowlist. Wins over a `from` definition's denylist.

editRegex.description: Confine THIS one-off worker's file edits to paths matching this regex (e.g. 'src/.*\\.ts$'). Enforced at the gate. Wins over a `from` definition's editRegex.

id.description: Stable short id for this criterion.

text.description: The checkable condition in plain language.

verify.description: Deterministic shell command that proves it, if any.

loop.description: Arm a dynamic loop on this worker AT SPAWN so it can't finish until the goal is provably met. strategy: command / judge / hybrid (default hybrid). limit: a positive number caps attempts; omit or null = unbounded.

text.description: The follow-up directive — becomes a new user-turn for the worker. State the new ask clearly and in full.

title.description: Short headline, a few words. E.g. 'Task complete'

body.description: One sentence with the concrete outcome. E.g. 'Auth refactor done across 3 workers — review ready.'

question.description: The complete question, ending with a question mark.

header.description: Short chip label, max ~12 chars. E.g. 'Approach'.

description.description: What this choice means / its trade-off.

options.description: 2-4 distinct choices. The dashboard adds a free-text 'Other' automatically.

questions.description: 1-4 questions, shown to the operator as one banner.

whenToUse.description: Routing signal: when should a task be dispatched to this worker.

persistent.description: Stay alive after a turn (conversational session).

toolsAllow.description: Tool allowlist (globs, e.g. 'Read', 'mcp__*'). Empty/omit ⇒ inherit all tools.

toolsDeny.description: Tool denylist (globs). Always subtracts, even from an allowlist.

editRegex.description: Restrict file edits to paths matching this regex (e.g. 'src/.*\\.ts$').

extends.description: Base worker name to inherit unset fields from.

body.description: This worker's role-instructions body (string). What to put in it / what NOT to (never restate the signal protocol / report / Handover) → create_worker's description and §Available workers.

ids.description: Limit to these worker ids. Omit to integrate ALL of your workers' branches.

op.description: 'attach' to arm a loop, 'stop' to end one.

target.description: Worker id to loop. Omit (or pass your own id) to loop yourself.

id.description: Stable short id for this criterion.

text.description: The checkable condition in plain language.

verify.description: Deterministic shell command that proves it, if any.

strategy.description: How the goal is checked. Defaults to 'hybrid'.

limit.description: Max attempts before the loop exhausts. null = unbounded (goal-met is the only exit).

loopId.description: The loop to stop (for 'stop'); omit to stop the target's active loop.

text.description: The report text. First line MUST be `result: ...`, `needs input: ...`, or `failed: ...` (this line is parsed). See the send_message_to_parent guidance for the structure of the lines that follow.

peerId.description: The id of the peer to consult (from list_peers) — for a peer you can already see.

peerName.description: The name of the peer to consult (the slug the orchestrator gave it, e.g. 'auth-expert'). Use this when the peer may not have spawned yet — the consult waits until a peer with this name joins. Pass peerId OR peerName, not both.

question.description: One focused, self-contained question, with the context the peer needs to answer.

answer.description: Your complete, self-contained answer to the peer request delivered this turn.
